titlestmt.titleproper: Legal documents - Kentucky, Virginia, North Carolina, Georgia4780
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-02-10 23:20:49 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Legal documents - Kentucky, Virginia, North Carolina, Georgia
did.abstract: Legal documents include wills, estate inventories, indentures and other land documents, summonses and other court documents, and accounts. Many relate to the ownership of slaves.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Legal documents include wills, estate inventories, indentures and other land documents, summonses and other court documents, and accounts. Many relate to the ownership of slaves.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Legal documents - Kentucky, Virginia, North Carolina, Georgia, #4780. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: North Carolina -- Social life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Slavery -- Georgia.
Slavery -- North Carolina.
Slavery -- Virginia.
Slavery -- Kentucky.
Slavery -- United States.
African Americans -- History -- To 1863.
African Americans -- Legal status, laws, etc. -- History -- 19th century.

did.unittitle: Kentucky 1781-1865
Two Documents. Virginia Court Case About Land in Kentucky, 1786-1792. Document About Sale of A horse and Wagon, ca. 1781, North Carolina
Slander Case Affidavit. Jane Hogan vs. Samuel Davis. Lincoln County, KY
Power of Attorney to Sell Kentucky Lands. Thomas Cunningham, James Adams
Removal of a Lawsuit. Green Clay. Lexington, Kentucky
Whiskey Accounts. Kentucky - Lexington
Three Documents. Levington Summons 1799. Levington Summons 1799. Fair Fax County, Virginia Summon 1798
Summons - Mason County 1799. Summons - Mason County 1800. Summons - Mason County 1801. Summons - Mason County 1807
Receipt for Land Purchase. James Taylor, 1805. Deed of General Warranty (Land) James Taylor, Campbell County, Kentucjky, 1814
Indentures (2). Land in Ohio
Power of Attorney George and Hugh Mercer. James Taylor. Campbell County, Kentucky
Richard Hickman. Lieutenant Governor. Clay County, Kentucky Land Grant
Autograph Letter. Edmond Penn
Deposition. Abraham Clay. About a slave named Amy Galbreath. Slavery. Mentions Cornwallis
Indenture. John Fowler. Includes Slaves. Lexington, Fayette County, Kentucky
Declaration. Hume vs. Childers. Horse Thief/Murder Case
Writ for Payment of Debt. Jane Clarke - Griffen Theobalds. Grant County, Kentucky
Power of Attorney. Samuel Adams, Sr. Shelby county, Kentucky
Document. Deed of Sale. Harrodsburg, Kentucky
Estate Inventory. Includes Slaves. Laurence Egbert. Mercer County, Kentucky
Decree of Division of Property Including Slaves
Estate Inventory. Slaves. William Fields. Mercer County, Kentucky
Will - Elizabeth Adams, 1833. Will - Phebe Fishback, 1837. Frees a Slave.
Estate Inventories (2). Mercer County, Kentucky
Indenture. William Holt and Joseph Holt. Transferring Property Including a Slave
Will-Hugh Evans. Mentions Slaves
Estate Inventories (2)
ALS From W. Washington to Taylor Thornton, New Post, Kentucky
Deposition From a Physician About Treating Slaves
Document by Governor Beriah Magoffin to Forgive Payment. Mercer County
Campbell Circuit Court. Newport, Kentucky. Order to Sell "Unruly" Slaves. Also Franklin County, Ohio
Oath of Office "Will not give aid to the rebellion" School Trustee
Illustrated Price List. C.P. Barnes and Brother, Louisville, Kentucky
Verginia 1750-1879
Estate Document, Printed. Fairfex County, Virginia
Will. Alexandria, Virginia
Complaints. Loudon County, VA 1759; Mason County, VA 1801; Campbell County KY 1815; Levi Todd 1798
Land Sale. Loudoun County, VA
Indenture. Loudoun County, VA
Service Indenture, Leesburg, VA; Land Transfer Loudon county, VA
Indenture For Servent; Land Rental, Loudoun County, VA
Land Indenture, Virginia
George Washington Documents: Bill of Sale - Washington Signature as Witness, Loudon County 1766; Warrant for Surveying Land for Washington, 1785; Land Survey 1787, 1788
Indenture, Loudoun County, VA
Landindenture, Loudoun County
Land Transfer, Fairfex County, VA
Indenture, Fayette County, VA 1783; Land Office Military Warrant 1784
Indentures, Fairfax County, VA
Summons (3): Lincoln County 1783, Madison County 1791, Lincoln County 1791
Indenture, Fairfax County, VA
Land Indentures: Fairfax County 1786, king George County 1791
Land Indenture, Loudon County, VA. "Blue Ridge" Deed
Indentures. Henry Lec, Westmore County VA
Land Transfers (2), Fairfax County
Land Transfers (2), Fairfax County
Ship Contract 1794, Proof or Imported Spirits 1804, Land Office Military Warrant 1810
Marriage Indenture, Fairfax VA
Peposition, Burke County 1795, Virginia Militia 1812
Indenture, Potomac Company and Canal of Great Falls, Fairfax County
Land Transfer, Fairfax County VA
Affidavit, Loudoun County
Deeds of Gift (2). Samuel Adams, Jr. Fairfax County VA
Indenture Fairfax/Loudoun Counties VA
Estate Settlement Including Slaves, Fairfax County VA
Amy Kilbread Trial Testimony
Sale of Slaves, Culpepper VA
ALS George F. Washington to Gen. James Taylor, Waverly, VA
Settlement Document, Fairfax County VA
Citizenship Declaration (Ireland) 1855; Complaint, Fairfax County VA 1846
Accounts, Patrick County, VA
Memorandum About an Interview With General Lee. May 9, 1862. General Henry A. Wise
State of Virginia Definition of Colored Person and Indians. Printed
Tobacco Sale Broadside. Danville, VA. Thos. L. Poindexter and Son
North Carolina 1824-1865
Listing of Goods for Sale. Roberts Jaffray and Co. Fayetteville, NC
Marriage Licenses, Lincoln and catawbe Counties, NC
Announcement of Opening of Boys' School in Melville, NC
Pardon by Henry Clark for Wesley Gray
Insurance Policy 1853, Signed Over 1857
Confederate States Receipt
Abstract of Forage
Georgia 1862-1872
Receipt for Purchase of Slave for $1000. Atlanta, Georgia
Clothing Appropriation Georgie Volunteers 1865, Confederate Visitor's Permit 1862
Clothing Appropriation, Georgia
Appointment and Receipt. Quartermaster/Agent Clothing. Georgia
Letter Refusing Any More Drafts Written on Advertisement for Grady's Company. To Ira Foster and Son. Savannah, GA
Freight Receipt. Hawkinsville Agency. Macon and Brunswick Railroad Co.
Place Unknown 1806-1846
Slave Receipt
Inventory
Estate Sale, Includes Slaves
